portfolio_rationale: LLY leads with unmatched fundamental quality (54% revenue growth, 83% gross margin, no bearish fundamental factors) despite near-term technical weakness, making it the highest-conviction long. AAPL earns the second-largest weight due to the cleanest combination of strong fundamentals and positive technical momentum with the weakest bear case. GOOGL gets a smaller allocation given overbought RSI (80) and declining search interest despite strong price momentum. AMZN and XOM are excluded or minimized due to persistent technical deterioration and weaker fundamental support respectively.

LLY.reason: Strongest fundamental case in the universe with 54% revenue growth, 83% gross margin, and no bearish fundamental factors; near-term technical weakness is a temporary headwind for a structurally superior business.

AAPL.reason: Cleanest bull case combining strong profitability metrics with positive technical momentum (price above 20/50 MA, MACD positive) and the weakest bear case of any ticker in the universe.

GOOGL.reason: Strong fundamentals and positive momentum but RSI at 80 signals overbought conditions and declining search interest warrants a reduced position relative to AAPL.

BRK.B.reason: Solid profitability and positive news sentiment but mixed technicals (price below 20-day MA, MACD below signal) and multiple recurring risk flags limit conviction.

AMZN.reason: Strong fundamentals are undermined by persistent technical deterioration (negative 5-day and 20-day returns, price below 20-day MA) and litigious news, warranting only a token position.

XOM.reason: Weakest fundamental case with only three bullish factors and no revenue growth data; bearish technical signals and recurring liquidity concerns limit upside conviction.
